preamble: The shift from the near-open front vowel [æ] to a closer front vowel like [e] (or a diphthong like [eə]) is a form of vowel raising. This is a prominent feature in several modern English dialect shifts, particularly before nasal consonants.

examples.note: The TRAP vowel is raised and tensed, a hallmark of the NCS.
Raising is especially common before nasal consonants.

sources: Labov, W., Ash, S., & Boberg, C. (2006). The Atlas of North American English. https://www.degruyterbrill.com/document/doi/10.1515/9783110167467/html?lang=en
